Functions for rendering health-related text boxes in a graphical user interface.

# Purpose
The code provides functionality for rendering text within a graphical user interface, specifically for a health-related application. It includes two main functions: [`health_ui_draw_text_in_box`](<#health_ui_draw_text_in_box>) and [`health_ui_render_typical_text_box`](<#health_ui_render_typical_text_box>). The [`health_ui_draw_text_in_box`](<#health_ui_draw_text_in_box>) function is responsible for drawing text within a specified rectangular area (`GRect`) on the screen, with options to customize the text and box colors, font, and alignment. It calculates the appropriate size and position for the text box, applies a border, and adjusts the text position to ensure proper display.

The [`health_ui_render_typical_text_box`](<#health_ui_render_typical_text_box>) function is designed to render a typical text box that displays a weekday label and a value text. It retrieves the current time, formats the weekday, and constructs a text string using internationalization support. The function then sets up a rectangular area for the text box, applies color settings, and draws the text with specified alignment and overflow handling. This code is part of a user interface module and relies on external libraries for graphics and internationalization, as indicated by the included headers.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `health_ui.h`
- `applib/pbl_std/pbl_std.h`
- `services/common/i18n/i18n.h`
- `util/string.h`


# Functions

---
### health\_ui\_draw\_text\_in\_box<!-- {{#callable:health_ui_draw_text_in_box}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/health/health_ui.c#L23>)

Draws text within a specified rectangular area on a graphical context, with optional background and text colors.
- **Inputs**:
    - `ctx`: A pointer to the `GContext` where the text and box will be drawn.
    - `text`: A constant character pointer to the text string to be drawn.
    - `drawing_bounds`: A [`GRect`](<../../../applib/graphics/gtypes.h.md#GRect>) structure defining the bounds of the drawing area.
    - `y_offset`: An integer specifying the vertical offset for the text box within the drawing bounds.
    - `small_font`: A `GFont` specifying the font to be used for the text.
    - `box_color`: A `GColor` specifying the color of the box background.
    - `text_color`: A `GColor` specifying the color of the text.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calculate the height of the text using `fonts_get_font_height` with the specified font.
    - Set the text overflow mode to `GTextOverflowModeFill` and alignment to `GTextAlignmentCenter`.
    - Define a [`GRect`](<../../../applib/graphics/gtypes.h.md#GRect>) for the text box using the drawing bounds and `y_offset`.
    - Calculate the content size of the text layout and adjust the horizontal position to center the text.
    - Inset the text fill box by 2 pixels to add a 3-pixel border.
    - Adjust the height and vertical position of the text fill box to correct for layout offsets.
    - If `box_color` is not `GColorClear`, set the fill color and draw the rectangle for the text box.
    - If `text_color` is not `GColorClear`, set the text color and draw the text within the text box.
- **Output**: No return value; the function performs drawing operations on the provided `GContext`.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`GRect`](<../../../applib/graphics/gtypes.h.md#GRect>)
    - [`app_graphics_text_layout_get_content_size`](<../../../applib/graphics/text_layout.c.md#app_graphics_text_layout_get_content_size>)
    - [`grect_inset`](<../../../applib/graphics/gtypes.c.md#grect_inset>)
    - [`GEdgeInsets`](<../../../applib/graphics/gtypes.h.md#GEdgeInsets>)

### health\_ui\_render\_typical\_text\_box<!-- {{#callable:health_ui_render_typical_text_box}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/health/health_ui.c#L57>)

Renders a text box with a typical weekday label and a given value text on a graphical context.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``ctx``: A pointer to the `GContext` object used for drawing.
    - ``layer``: A pointer to the `Layer` object that provides the bounds for the text box.
    - ``value_text``: A constant character pointer to the text string to display in the text box.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Get the current time and convert it to a `struct tm` format.
    - Extract the current weekday as a string and convert it to uppercase.
    - Format the `typical_text` string with the weekday and a localized 'TYPICAL' prefix.
    - Determine the vertical position `y` for the text box based on the display type (rectangular or not).
    - Create a [`GRect`](<../../../applib/graphics/gtypes.h.md#GRect>) for the text box with calculated dimensions and apply insets if the display is rectangular.
    - Set the background and text colors based on whether the display supports color.
    - Set the font to `FONT_KEY_GOTHIC_18_BOLD`.
    - Fill a rounded rectangle with the background color in the specified [`GRect`](<../../../applib/graphics/gtypes.h.md#GRect>).
    - Adjust the [`GRect`](<../../../applib/graphics/gtypes.h.md#GRect>) to draw the first line of text and set the text color.
    - Draw the `typical_text` in the adjusted [`GRect`](<../../../applib/graphics/gtypes.h.md#GRect>) with word wrap and center alignment.
    - Move the [`GRect`](<../../../applib/graphics/gtypes.h.md#GRect>) down to draw the `value_text` below the `typical_text`.
    - Draw the `value_text` in the adjusted [`GRect`](<../../../applib/graphics/gtypes.h.md#GRect>) with word wrap and center alignment.
- **Output**: No return value; the function performs drawing operations on the provided `GContext`.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`localtime_r`](<../../../util/time/time.c.md#localtime_r>)
    - [`strftime`](<../../../applib/pbl_std/strftime.c.md#strftime>)
    - [`toupper_str`](<../../../../libutil/string.c.md#toupper_str>)
    - [`GRect`](<../../../applib/graphics/gtypes.h.md#GRect>)
    - [`grect_inset`](<../../../applib/graphics/gtypes.c.md#grect_inset>)
    - [`GEdgeInsets`](<../../../applib/graphics/gtypes.h.md#GEdgeInsets>)
